scope: LLMs, prompt engineering, RAG, models, MLOps, AI tools, knowledge graphs / GraphDB as AI harness, ontology engineering for AI/LLM systems. Technical and foundational content about how AI/ML systems work.
Programming languages, CS algorithms, operating systems (Linux / WSL), Git, dev tools, cloud infrastructure, software architecture.
Computer architecture, chips, semiconductors as engineering, electronics, devices, silicon design.
Pure and applied mathematics, probability, statistics, formal logic, decision theory, computational theory foundations.
Life sciences, genetics, cellular biology, evolution, organisms.
Physical sciences, astronomy, cosmology, chemistry at fundamental level, materials physics.
Brain mechanisms, cognitive science, consciousness studies, philosophy of mind. Content that maps cognitive phenomena to neurological substrates.

Scientific or technical content that does not fit cleanly in #1–7. Use ONLY when you can articulate in one sentence why none of #1–7 applies. If you cannot articulate that reason, re-examine #1–7 first.
Investment philosophy, methods, mental models in the Buffett / Munger / Li Lu / Pabrai tradition.

Applied personal-investor finance: sector analysis (mining, oil & gas, software, semis as industry), company analysis, equity research methodology, retirement financial planning, tax strategies, portfolio construction.
Macro, monetary policy, currency, bonds, interest rates, gold, commodities, market structure, economic data and statistics (GDP series, unemployment, inflation, central bank data, market indicators).
Strategy, operations, organizational design — distinct from investment-decision-making.
Nutrition, fitness, longevity, sleep, applied medicine — life-application angle.
Historical events, civilizations, eras, historical biographies, completed historical periods.
International relations, geopolitical analysis, current world events, demographics, world powers.
Cognitive psychology, behavior, personal development, productivity.
Religions, theology, comparative religion, meditation, spiritual practice.
Fiction, poetry, literary criticism, author studies.
Western and Eastern philosophy, ethics, epistemology, metaphysics.
Visual arts, architecture, design (graphic, industrial, interaction, residential), music — aesthetic and creative disciplines.
Travel, collections, food and drinks, retirement activities and goals, home design choices, and other content about how to live well — the personal-experience-of-living dimension.

Standalone quotes — attributable one-liners, wisdom snippets, aphorisms. Use ONLY when the source's primary unit is a standalone quotation or quote collection. Quote-rich essays, speeches, or books classify by substantive subject.

Residual catch-all for genuinely uncategorizable content. Use ONLY when no other domain in this list describes the content's primary nature. High `undecided` rates indicate the domain list needs expansion.
